hero.headline.value: Senior software engineer. I ship systems where agents write the code — and I built the tooling to trust them in production.

hero.intro.value: I'm Bobby. I became a software engineer by chasing the parts of code that feel like math — type systems, proofs, recursion, the self-referential knots where a thing contains itself and still holds together. I go down those paths because they're beautiful, not because anyone asked; the useful part shows up on its own. Lately it showed up as agents.

hero.summary.value: At Aura I ship agent-authored delivery in production. The instinct behind it is old — make invalid states unrepresentable, push correctness into the type system until violations can't compile — and agents are just where it turned urgent: orgs want them shipping like senior engineers and give them none of the safety rails. So I built the rails — a contract-and-validation layer where every privileged effect is bound to a signed owner, enforced in CI. The harder, general version is out in the open, in prx and the bounded-systems libraries.

seeking.focus.value: Engineering on agentic products · safe agent-authored delivery · agent infrastructure

seeking.detail.value: Senior engineering on teams building agentic products — where having shipped agent-authored systems and their guardrails is the rare part. Brooklyn · NYC · remote.

exp.bounded-systems.what.value: Capability security for AI agents — software delivered by agents, governed like infrastructure. The open, general form of the guardrails I ship at Aura.

exp.bounded-systems.b0.value: Designed a capability runtime — prx, guest-room, claude-box — where agents act only through the scoped door references they hold, every privileged effect is verified against its signed owner, and provenance is bound to an auditable chain (ocap-provenance).

exp.aura.what.value: On Commerce — the team behind Aura's website, commerce-routing, and business-operations systems — I architected the contract-and-validation layer behind inventory-purchasing: one source of truth, CI drift-gated across 130+ components, delivered as agent-authored PRs.

exp.aura.b0.value: Architected the contract-and-validation layer replacing Aura's spreadsheet-driven supply plan (the system behind inventory-purchasing): a compile-and-publish model where one source of truth generates every downstream surface — schemas, docs, tests, agent context — CI drift-gated so nothing falls out of sync.

exp.aura.b1.value: Made the pipeline auditable by construction — provenance and contract checks gating every generated output, sign-off bound to an owner-role enforced in CI rather than to individuals.

exp.aura.b2.value: Drove it as agent-authored delivery: every change moves through one auditable pipeline to a reviewed PR, applying the same provenance-and-contract discipline to the agents' own output.

exp.l2l.what.value: Built the quality gates and developer experience for a 20-engineer team — static-analysis PR gating that blocked defects before merge, on top of design-system and performance work.

exp.l2l.b0.value: Built static-analysis gating into the PR pipeline — blocking defects before merge, cutting critical bugs 20% and high-priority issues 25% across a 20-engineer team.

exp.l2l.b1.value: Shipped the frontend platform behind it — a design system (−30% task lead time), a dashboard framework (widget builds a week → a day), and pagination + perf work that brought First Contentful Paint under a second.

exp.pioneer-works.what.value: Built the events + revenue data layer for the arts foundation.

exp.pioneer-works.b0.value: A unified CRM + Square-API ETL — −80% payment-tracking time, P&L across 200+ events.

exp.kaleida-studio.what.value: Built just-in-time production automation for the fabrication studio.

exp.kaleida-studio.b0.value: Just-in-time production workflows — eliminated edition-numbering errors (10/month → 0).

exp.the-prepared.what.value: Built the publishing pipeline for the manufacturing publication.

exp.the-prepared.b0.value: A unified content pipeline — −70% curation time across 1000+ articles.

exp.recurse-center.what.value: Type theory in Haskell — using the type system to make invalid states unrepresentable. The intellectual root of the capability work: push correctness into the boundary so violations can't compile.

exp.recurse-center.b0.value: Algebraic data types, typeclasses, and type-driven design — the same instinct now applied to security seams: invalid states unrepresentable, sign-off un-forgeable.

work.prx.value: The agent-run work-unit CLI: capability-scoped agents whose every privileged effect is verified against its signed owner, driving a work unit through one signed pipeline to a merged PR.

work.guest-room.value: Guest-agnostic room+door capability runtime — an agent reaches the outside world only through the door references it's handed.

work.claude-box.value: A capability-secured box for agent sessions — its authority is the door references it holds (keeper/scout/concierge/net), parent-agnostic.

work.door-kit.value: In-box door-client SDK for claude-box's capability doors (keeper/scout/concierge/spawn), over the guest-room protocol.

work.ocap-provenance.value: Capability-use provenance — a schema + SLSA mapping binding each privileged effect to a signed owner and an auditable chain.

work.string-audit.value: Cost-aware, grounded content auditor — typed string symbols, type-scoped audits, CAS-memoized LLM calls.
